 {"id":"bd-1hkm","title":"Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler (pi.tool)","description":"# Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Tool that routes pi.tool() calls from extensions to the Rust ToolRegistry.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.tool("read", {path: "foo.txt"}), the flow is:
1. JS: pi.tool() enqueues HostcallRequest with kind=Tool
2. Rust: Dispatcher drains request, extracts tool name + params
3. Rust: Looks up tool in ToolRegistry, calls execute()
4. Rust: Converts ToolOutput to HostcallOutcome
5. JS: Promise resolves with result

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.tool(name, input) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// This allows extensions to invoke any tool in the registry,
    /// including built-in tools and other extension-registered tools.
    async fn dispatch_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        // Find tool in registry
        let Some(tool) = self.tool_registry.get(name) else {
            return HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_NOT_FOUND".to_string(),
                message: format!("Tool '{}' not found in registry", name),
            };
        };
        
        // Generate unique tool_call_id for tracking
        let tool_call_id = format!("ext-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string());
        
        // Execute the tool
        match tool.execute(&tool_call_id, input, None).await {
            Ok(output) => {
                // Convert ToolOutput to JSON Value
                let result = serde_json::json!({
                    "content": output.content,
                    "details": output.details,
                });
                HostcallOutcome::Success(result)
            }
            Err(e) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

| Error Condition | Code | Behavior |
|-----------------|------|----------|
| Tool not found | TOOL_NOT_FOUND | Return error outcome |
| Tool execution fails | TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR | Return error with message |
| Invalid params | INVALID_PARAMS | Return validation error |
| Timeout | TIMEOUT | Return timeout error |

## Integration with Update Callbacks

The TypeScript reference supports streaming tool updates via the onUpdate callback. For v1, we can omit this (return final result only). Future enhancement:

```rust
// Future: Support streaming updates
let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(16);
let update_callback = Some(Box::new(move |update| {
    let _ = tx.try_send(update);
}));
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: Tool found and executed successfully
2. Unit test: Tool not found returns error
3. Unit test: Tool execution error propagates
4. Integration test: Built-in tool (read) via extension
5. Integration test: Extension-registered tool via another extension

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Blocks: End-to-end extension tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_tool() method implemented
- [ ] Tool lookup in registry works
- [ ] Successful execution returns Success outcome
- [ ] Errors return Error outcome with code + message
- [ ] Tool call IDs are unique
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.871031814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199266466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199200443Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtensionDispatcher dispatch_and_complete() for HostcallKind::Tool using ToolRegistry; added unit tests covering success + unknown tool rejection; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.885917174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:48.505416010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1i5x","title":"Implement pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() for message injection","description":"Allow extensions to inject messages into the conversation.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
// Custom message (not for LLM, optional display)
pi.sendMessage({
  customType: string,
  content: string,
  display?: boolean,
  details?: unknown
}, {
  triggerTurn?: boolean,
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp' | 'nextTurn'
});

// User message (triggers LLM turn)
pi.sendUserMessage(content, {
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp'
});
```

## Delivery Modes
- **steer**: Interrupt after current tool, skip remaining tool calls
- **followUp**: Queue after agent finishes current turn
- **nextTurn**: Default, processed as next user input

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for 'sendMessage' and 'sendUserMessage'
2. Integration with agent message queues (steering_queue, follow_up_queue)
3. Custom message type in session entries
4. Display logic in TUI for custom messages

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark', content: 'Saved\!'})
2. Hostcall creates CustomMessage entry
3. If display=true, shows in TUI
4. If triggerTurn=true, queues for processing

## Test Plan
- Unit test: sendMessage creates correct entry type
- Unit test: sendUserMessage triggers agent turn
- Integration test: steer mode interrupts current processing
- Integration test: followUp mode queues correctly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/extensions_js.rs (bridge to Rust)
- src/agent.rs (message queue integration)
- src/interactive.rs (display custom messages)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.477300174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777896986Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777832025Z","close_reason":"pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() both fully implemented as hostcalls in src/extensions.rs (line ~5531 and ~5606). ExtensionHostActions trait defines both methods. -{"id":"bd-1ilq","title":"Implement pi.registerShortcut() for extension keyboard bindings","description":"Allow extensions to register custom keyboard shortcuts.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerShortcut(key, {
  description: string,
  handler: (ctx) => Promise<void>
});
```

Key format: 'ctrl+u', 'alt+shift+x', 'f1', etc.

## Implementation Requirements
1. ShortcutRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerShortcut'
3. Integration with keybindings system (src/keybindings.rs)
4. Validation that key isn't reserved (interrupt, clear, exit, suspend)
5. Shortcut execution via extension runtime

## Reserved Keys (cannot be overridden)
- ctrl+c (interrupt)
- ctrl+d (exit)
- ctrl+l (clear)
- ctrl+z (suspend)

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+u', {...})
2. Hostcall validates key not reserved, stores in registry
3. KeyMsg in bubbletea checked against extension shortcuts
4. If match, invoke JS handler with ExtensionContext

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerShortcut stores shortcut
- Unit test: reserved keys rejected
- Integration test: registered shortcut triggers handler

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ShortcutRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/keybindings.rs (integrate with extension shortcuts)
- src/interactive.rs (dispatch to extension handler on key)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.738747397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:25:17.966186896Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.745921073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:32.254783057Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1iwi","title":"Workstream: Interactive Editor UX Parity (autocomplete, multiline, bang, images)","description":"# Goal
Bring the **interactive editor experience** to parity with legacy pi-mono, focusing on:
- Autocomplete (commands, templates, skills, file references, path completion)
- Multi-line editing behavior (Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits)
- Bash shortcuts (`!` and `!!`)
- Clipboard image paste + drag/drop attachments

# Legacy Behavior (Source-of-Truth)
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Editor”:
- File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files
- Path completion: Tab to complete paths
- Multi-line: Shift+Enter (Ctrl+Enter on Windows Terminal)
- Images: Ctrl+V to paste, or drag onto terminal
- Bash commands: `!command` runs and sends output to LLM, `!!command` runs without sending

Legacy implementation details:
- Autocomplete is provided by `CombinedAutocompleteProvider` (slash commands, prompt templates, extension commands, skill commands, file path completion using `fd`).
- Image paste writes clipboard image to a temp file and inserts the file path into the editor.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/interactive.rs` reserves Tab for future autocomplete but has no autocomplete UI.
- No `@` file fuzzy search.
- No Shift+Enter newline semantics (current behavior differs).
- No `!` / `!!` bash shortcuts.
- No clipboard image paste handler.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Autocomplete engine
- Provide suggestions for:
  - Slash commands (built-in)
  - Prompt templates (`/<template>`)
  - Skill commands (`/skill:name`) when enabled
  - File references after typing `@` (fuzzy search project files)
  - Path completion when cursor is inside a path token

## 2) Autocomplete UI
- Render a dropdown/list with:
  - configurable max visible items (`autocomplete_max_visible`)
  - keyboard navigation
  - selection/accept behavior

## 3) Multi-line input semantics
- Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits.
- Windows Terminal: Ctrl+Enter inserts newline.

## 4) Bash shortcuts
- `!cmd`: run and include output in LLM context.
- `!!cmd`: run but do not include output in LLM context.

## 5) Clipboard image paste and drag/drop
- Ctrl+V attempts to read clipboard image:
  - if an image exists, write it to a temp file and insert the file path at cursor.
  - do nothing (silent) if clipboard has no image or access denied.
- Drag/drop: terminals usually paste a file path; ensure we handle file paths cleanly.

# Testing
- Unit tests for autocomplete matching and insertion.
- State tests for key interactions (Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+V).
- Integration tests for `!` / `!!` behavior (using deterministic command runner where possible).

# Dependencies
- Keybinding routing (`bd-3ip`) for Tab / Shift+Enter / Ctrl+V behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits
[ ] ! and !! bash shortcuts implemented
[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)

 {"id":"bd-1iz5","title":"Error UX: define hint taxonomy + mapping (Error -> hints)","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical mapping from internal error variants to user-facing remediation hints.

# Scope
- Enumerate the main error variants surfaced to users (config/auth/provider/tools/session/resources).
- For each variant, define:
  - a 1-line summary (human readable)
  - 0-2 actionable hints (commands, env vars, paths)
  - any contextual fields to print (file path, provider name, status code)

# Notes / Guidance
- Prefer stable phrasing so tests can assert on it.
- Prefer OS-specific hints only when we can detect OS reliably.
- Avoid suggesting destructive actions.

# Deliverable
- A documented table (in code or in `docs/troubleshooting.md`) that future contributors can extend.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mapping covers the top ~20 user-facing failure modes.

 {"id":"bd-3j4f","title":"Rustdoc: audit public API surface (decide what is truly public)","description":"# Goal
Clarify the intended public API of the `pi` crate so rustdoc work is focused and we don’t accidentally commit to unstable internals.

# Scope
- Inventory `pub` modules/types in `src/lib.rs` and key modules.
- Decide for each:
  - should remain `pub` and documented as supported
  - should become `pub(crate)` / private
  - should be re-exported from a smaller facade module

# Why
A smaller, intentional public surface reduces maintenance burden and makes rustdoc more meaningful.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A short list of "public" modules/types exists (can be a comment in `src/lib.rs` or a doc note).

 {"id":"bd-3ja5","title":"Task: Create ExtensionToolWrapper implementing Tool trait","description":"# Task: Create ExtensionToolWrapper

## Objective

Create a Rust struct that wraps an extension-registered tool, implementing the Tool trait so it can be used in the agent's tool registry.

## Implementation

```rust
/// Wrapper for extension-registered tools.
/// 
/// This struct implements the Tool trait, allowing extension tools
/// to be used alongside built-in tools in the agent's registry.
pub struct ExtensionToolWrapper {
    def: ExtensionToolDef,
    runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
}

impl ExtensionToolWrapper {
    pub fn new(def: ExtensionToolDef, runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>) -> Self {
        Self { def, runtime }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for ExtensionToolWrapper {
    fn name(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.name
    }
    
    fn label(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.label
    }
    
    fn description(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.description
    }
    
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        self.def.parameters.clone()
    }
    
    async fn execute(
        &self,
        tool_call_id: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
        _on_update: Option<Box<dyn Fn(ToolUpdate) + Send>>,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        // Call into JS runtime to execute the tool
        self.runtime.execute_extension_tool(
            &self.def.name,
            tool_call_id,
            input,
        ).await
    }
}
```

## JS Bridge Method

Need corresponding method in PiJsRuntime:

```rust
impl PiJsRuntime {
    /// Execute an extension-registered tool.
    pub async fn execute_extension_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        call_id: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        let input_json = serde_json::to_string(&input)?;
        
        // Call JS handler
        let result = self.eval(&format!(
            "__pi_execute_tool({}, {}, {})",
            serde_json::to_string(name)?,
            serde_json::to_string(call_id)?,
            input_json
        )).await?;
        
        // Wait for async result (tool handlers are async)
        // This requires Promise resolution via the hostcall mechanism
        // The tool handler calls back via the Promise bridge
        
        // Parse result
        self.await_tool_result(call_id).await
    }
}
```

## Async Tool Execution

Extension tools are async in JS:
```javascript
registerTool({
    name: "my-tool",
    execute: async (callId, input) => {
        // Async work...
        return { content: [...], details: {...} };
    }
});
```

The execution flow:
1. Rust calls __pi_execute_tool(name, callId, input)
2. JS looks up handler and calls it (returns Promise)
3. Promise resolves to tool result
4. Result marshaled back to Rust

## Testing

1. Unit test: Wrapper implements Tool trait correctly
2. Unit test: execute() routes to JS
3. Unit test: JS result parsed correctly
4. Integration test: Full tool invocation via wrapper

- Depends on: bd-254u (get_registered_tools)

 {"id":"bd-3kud","title":"Bash tool: timeout should kill process before drain","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:54:50.777485206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:14.117695745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:14.117632978Z","close_reason":"Fix: run_bash_command kills process on timeout before draining output","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3lj5","title":"Implement pi.registerFlag() for extension CLI flags","description":"Allow extensions to register custom CLI flags.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerFlag(name, {
  description: string,
  type: 'boolean' | 'string',
  default?: boolean | string
});

// Later retrieval:
const value = pi.getFlag('my-flag');
```

## Implementation Requirements
1. FlagRegistry in extension runtime
2. Two-phase CLI parsing (like legacy):
   - First pass: parse extension/skill/prompt/theme flags, load resources
   - Second pass: parse with extension-registered flags
3. Hostcall handlers for 'registerFlag' and 'getFlag'
4. Integration with clap argument parsing

## Challenges
- Flags must be registered before CLI parsing completes
- Extension loading happens early in startup
- Need to re-parse args after extensions register flags

## Data Flow
1. Extensions loaded early (from settings + CLI flags)
2. Extension calls pi.registerFlag('my-flag', {...})
3. CLI re-parses with registered flags
4. Extension can call pi.getFlag('my-flag') to get value

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerFlag stores flag definition
- Unit test: getFlag returns correct value
- Integration test: --my-flag value parsed correctly
- E2E test: pi --my-flag value runs with extension seeing value

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (FlagRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/cli.rs (two-phase parsing)
- src/main.rs (wire up flag registration timing)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:01.155543842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:33.365838748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lj5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:01.171934322Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lj5","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:33.365802110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3lv3","title":"Implement pi.events for inter-extension communication","description":"Allow extensions to communicate via a shared event bus.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
const eventBus = pi.events;

// Emit custom event
eventBus.emit(eventName: string, data: unknown): void

// Listen for events
const unsubscribe = eventBus.on(eventName: string, handler: (data) => void): () => void
```

## Use Cases
- Extension coordination (one extension signals another)
- Workflow automation (chain of extensions)
- State sharing between extensions

## Implementation Requirements
1. SharedEventBus in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handlers for 'events.emit' and 'events.on'
3. Handler registration with unsubscribe support
4. Event dispatch to all registered handlers

## Concurrency Notes
- Multiple handlers may fire concurrently
- Handlers execute in JS runtime context
- Errors in one handler shouldn't affect others

## Test Plan
- Unit test: emit triggers registered handlers
- Unit test: unsubscribe stops handler calls
- Unit test: multiple handlers receive same event
- Integration test: two extensions communicate

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (SharedEventBus)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handlers)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:54.533323213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:11.087167036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:11.087100983Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: JS-side __pi_events_native in extensions_js.rs (emit/list ops), Rust-side dispatch_events in extension_dispatcher.rs:417-493 handles list and emit, event handlers tracked in __pi_hook_index.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lv3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:54.543509825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lv3","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:41.975183855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m7f","title":"Workstream: Documentation Parity (core user docs)","description":"# Goal
Port and adapt the **core user documentation** from legacy pi-mono into this Rust repo so that:
- Users can install/configure/run Pi without reading legacy docs.
- Interactive commands and keybindings are documented accurately.
- Session/tree semantics are clear and match implementation.

# Background
Legacy docs live in:
`legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/*`

This repo currently has strong extension documentation and test matrices, but is missing most end-user docs (settings, keybindings, sessions, tree navigation, TUI, providers, packages, etc.).

# Scope
Create/port the following docs into `docs/` (Rust-adapted):
- `docs/keybindings.md`
- `docs/settings.md`
- `docs/session.md`
- `docs/tree.md`
- `docs/tui.md`
- `docs/themes.md`
- `docs/rpc.md`
- `docs/providers.md`
- `docs/models.md`
- `docs/skills.md`
- `docs/prompt-templates.md`
- `docs/packages.md`
- `docs/terminal-setup.md`
- `docs/development.md`
- `docs/windows.md`
- `docs/termux.md`

# Guidelines
- Docs must reflect **Rust implementation reality** (flags, paths, defaults) while preserving legacy intent.
- Where behavior is not yet implemented, mark clearly and link to the bead ID that tracks it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Docs exist in this repo and are coherent without legacy references.
- [ ] All interactive commands have doc coverage.
- [ ] Docs mention where config files live and how precedence works.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Core user docs updated to match current CLI/TUI behavior and commands

[ ] Documentation verification (link check or doc test script) emits JSONL logs + artifact index per bd-4u9

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:47:56.577402486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:36:12.419161976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:37:22.998808214Z","close_reason":"Docs parity workstream complete (all core docs present; added slash command surface list to docs/tui.md)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3m7f","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-3m7f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added `docs/troubleshooting.md` bead: bd-3oa9 (depends on error hint mapping bd-1iz5 so wording stays consistent with on-screen hints).","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:25:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs
- tests/common/logging.rs
- tests/common/harness.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger captures all levels
- [ ] Context key-values supported
- [ ] Timestamps included
- [ ] Redaction helpers present
- [ ] Artifact registry present
- [ ] Auto-dump on test failure
- [ ] Optional file output works
- [ ] All integration tests use harness","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.070756084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:50.752577524Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124430233Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction helpers, artifact registry + dump, and TEST_LOG_PATH file output in test logging; harness now dumps artifacts on panic and provides record_artifact. Acceptance criteria satisfied.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ml","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-3ml","author":"QuietBear","text":"Reviewed existing tests/common logger/harness: core logging already present but missing redaction, artifact registry, and TEST_LOG_PATH dump. Implementing those now in tests/common/logging.rs + tests/common/harness.rs (record_artifact, dump_artifacts, redaction helper, env-based file output, artifact dump on panic).","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:22Z"}]}
